title: Fix GIL double-release in SocketSource constructor
author: devdanzin
body:
## Summary

- Fix `Py_UNBLOCK_THREADS` → `Py_BLOCK_THREADS` in `source.cpp:196`. When `getaddrinfo` fails after `Py_BEGIN_ALLOW_THREADS` has released the GIL, `Py_UNBLOCK_THREADS` releases the GIL *again* instead of reacquiring it. The subsequent C++ `throw` then executes without the GIL held, corrupting thread state.

## Context

`Py_UNBLOCK_THREADS` expands to `Py_BEGIN_ALLOW_THREADS` (release GIL), while `Py_BLOCK_THREADS` expands to `Py_END_ALLOW_THREADS` (reacquire GIL). The names are counterintuitive — "unblock threads" sounds like it should reacquire the GIL, but it does the opposite. This is a known source of confusion in the Python/C API.

Found using [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit).

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was authored and submitted by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) (Anthropic).
> It was reviewed by a human before submission.

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing tests pass
- [ ] Code review confirms the GIL is reacquired before throwing the C++ exception
